:::: {.panel-tabset .nav-pills} ## Parole Eligibility
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

How is Parole Release Eligibility Determined?

In Texas, most inmates are eligible for release on parole when their time served plus good conduct time equals one-fourth of the sentence imposed or 15 years, whichever is less. Inmates who are convicted of consecutive felony sentences may not be released until they are eligible for parole on the last sentence imposed. Inmates who have never been convicted of certain sexual or violent crimes may be given a presumptive parole date at or after their initial parole eligibility date. Texas inmates may also be eligible for mandatory supervision release, and the Board shall release them, “when the actual calendar time the inmate has served plus any accrued good conduct time equals the term to which the inmate was sentenced.” However, the Board may deny mandatory supervision release to inmates if board members believe that “good conduct time is not an accurate reflection of the inmate’s potential for rehabilitation,” and that therefore “the inmate’s release would endanger public safety.”


People who were Eligible for Parole

In 2020, Hispanic, any race people constituted the most number of people eligible for parole but still in prison, accounting for 34% (17,761 people) of the parole-eligible prison population.



In 2020, there were 8,371 people who were incarcerated for a parole revocation and were eligible for parole but not yet released from prison.

``` ::: :::
::: {.cell} :::
## Prison Population
::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Prison Population

In 2020, there were 51,739 people who were eligible for parole but not released from prison, constituting 42% of the parole-eligible prison population.




``` ::: :::

## Offenses ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}

Most Serious Sentenced Offense

In 2020, there were 22,274 people who were parole eligible but still in prison for non-violent offenses, accounting for 43% of the parole-eligible prison population.
